questions.title: Top Priority: Landry's Record
questions.prompt: Which of the following is the most important priority for evaluating Governor Landry's record and promises? (Rank #1 response)

questions.title: Belief: Gambling Benefits Louisiana
questions.prompt: Legalizing online casino gaming would benefit Louisiana's economy through jobs and tax revenue.

questions.title: Belief: Offshore Already Happening
questions.prompt: Offshore online casino gambling is already happening in Louisiana — legalizing it would just bring it under state control.

questions.title: Belief: Money Leaves the State
questions.prompt: Money that Louisiana residents spend on offshore gambling is leaving the state rather than staying in Louisiana.

questions.title: Belief: Parish Opt-Out
questions.prompt: Parishes that have previously voted against gambling should be allowed to opt out of online casino gaming.

questions.title: Belief: Tribes-Only Is Unfair
questions.prompt: Limiting online casino gaming licenses only to Louisiana tribes is unfair to other operators.

questions.title: Belief: Tribal Gaming Keeps Revenue in Louisiana
questions.prompt: Having Louisiana tribes operate online casino gaming means the revenue stays in Louisiana rather than going to out-of-state corporations.

questions.title: Belief: Apps Designed to Be Addictive
questions.prompt: Online casino gambling apps are designed to be addictive and make it harder for people to control their spending.

questions.title: Belief: Adult Freedom
questions.prompt: Adults in Louisiana should be free to make their own choices about legal forms of entertainment, including online casino gaming.

questions.title: Belief: State Protects Families
questions.prompt: The state has a responsibility to protect Louisiana families from the harms of gambling addiction.

questions.title: Argument Matchup: Corporate Fairness vs. Tribal Revenue
questions.prompt: Which argument is more convincing to you?

options.label: Allowing only Louisiana's tribes to operate online casino gaming is unfair. It gives one group of operators a special deal and shuts other companies out of the market.
The companies calling this "unfair" are big corporate gaming operators based outside of Louisiana. Letting Louisiana tribes operate online casino gaming means that more money stays in Louisiana.

state wide.label: Allowing only Louisiana's tribes to operate online casino gaming is unfair. It gives one group of operators a special deal and shuts other companies out of the market.
The companies calling this "unfair" are big corporate gaming operators based outside of Louisiana. Letting Louisiana tribes operate online casino gaming means that more money stays in Louisiana.

questions.title: Argument Matchup: Addiction Risk vs. Consumer Safeguards
questions.prompt: Which argument is more convincing to you?

options.label: Online casino gambling apps are designed to be addictive, and would put a casino in every Louisiana smartphone, making it easier for kids and addicted Louisianans to lose money their families can't afford. Governor Landry would be putting tax revenue ahead of Louisiana families.

Illegal offshore gambling sites already operate in Louisiana today with no rules. A regulated tribal-state framework would add age verification, consumer protections, enforcement, and safeguards that do not exist today.

questions.title: Argument Matchup: Voter Say vs. Regulated Tribal Framework
questions.prompt: Which argument is more convincing to you?

options.label: Opponents say this would let the Governor approve a major gambling expansion without voters having a direct say, and could open the door to online gambling in communities that have voted against gambling.
Supporters say this is not a gambling free-for-all. It is a limited, regulated tribal framework with strict guardrails aimed at replacing illegal offshore activity.

questions.title: Trusted Messengers on iGaming
questions.prompt: Which of the following voices would be most influential in shaping your opinion on online casino gaming in Louisiana?

modes.label: What respondents with a view said

response_universe.explainer: In "What respondents with a view said", we remove Don't know / Neither responses and rescale the remaining responses to sum to 100%.

key_takeaways: A majority of Louisiana voters have gambled in some form in the past year, most commonly through the state lottery or visiting a casino.
Most voters are unaware that offshore online casino gambling is available to Louisianans today — awareness is a key context for the legalization debate.
Voters broadly agree that money is leaving the state to offshore sites and that adults should have freedom of choice, but also express concern about addiction and family protection.
The regulation-and-consumer-safeguards frame beats the addiction-risk frame; adult freedom beats state paternalism.
Roads and bridges top the list of preferred uses for iGaming revenue, with tax relief and law enforcement close behind.
A substantial share of voters are open to persuasion; local law enforcement and independent economists rank as the most trusted messengers.
content.methodology_note: We estimate a machine learning ensemble that predicts opinion as a function of party, geography, age, sex, education, income, religion, race, and contextual variables. We generate predicted probabilities for detailed demographic cells and post-stratify them to the statewide and cultural-region levels using turnout-adjusted population weights.
